Mtn To Limit Expert Witness Fees; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Feb-27-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Robert S. Green/Jenelle Welling, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & moving party: Ronald Elijah
Robert Ward/Kojsa Minor, Esq., appeared for Defendant & opposing party: The Information Age Fund, et al

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff Ronald Elijah's Motion To Limit Expert Witness Fees is granted as the court sets the reasonable fee for Mr. Nils H. Hakansson at $1,000 per hour. Mr. Hakansson is being paid at the rate of $1,500 for his services by Defendant and by other entities. However, the inquiry to be made by the court is defined in Marsh v Mountain Zephyr, Inc. 43 Cal.App.4th 289, 295: "... the court wishes to make clear that its ruling is not to be construed as a holding that Mr. Stevenson's charges are unreasonable as to his clients. Rather, it is limited to what must be paid by parties who did not hire him and who are compelled to depose him." In making this order, the court has made the same inquiry and has determined that it is unreasonable to put that burden on Plaintiff. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Robert Charles Ward of Shartsis Friese, LLP, 421-6500, appearing for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified: DEFENDANT RS INVESTMENT TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to causes of action 1, 2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 10, 11, and sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action 3, and 6. The Court modified its ruling with regard to Cause of action 9, which is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Definition of "written contract" preserves ambiguity as to whether complaint based on oral promises. Delayed discovery allegations must explain why relevant facts were not and could not have been discovered earlier. Contract claims may be asserted only against parties to contract. Tolling agreement applies only to claims against its signatories. Fraud based claims must plead reliance on alleged misrepresentations. The Court anticipates this will be the final opportunity to correct pleading deficiencies. Any amended complaint must comply with CRC 312 (g) and CCP 128.7 (b).
